title: Get a permanent resident card - Canada.ca
content:
## Get a permanent resident card
A permanent resident (PR) card shows that you’re a permanent resident of Canada. You need it to return to Canada after travelling.
Find out how to submit an application to
- get your first PR card
- renew an expired or expiring card
- report or replace a lost card
- update the information on your card, such as your name

## If you’re not already a permanent resident
You must first apply for permanent residence and be approved before you can get a PR card.
Explore our immigration programs.

## If you’re not already a permanent resident
You must first apply for permanent residence and be approved before you can get a PR card.
Explore our immigration programs.
Error loading fee. Check the fee list .
Your first card won’t cost anything if you send your photo and address within 180 days of becoming a permanent resident.
New PR card: Error loading the number of days. Try again.
Renewal: Error loading the number of days. Try
            again.
Processing times will vary based on
- the type of application submitted
- if the application is complete
- how quickly we expect to process applications we’ve already received
- how easily we can verify your information
- how long you take to respond to any requests or concerns
Learn how processing times are calculated .
Learn about your first tax year, benefits and credits.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
